source-discipline.reason: Every number in the Headline Outcomes table and the Artifacts Touched table is derived from the session's own tool output (DB queries, file system scans, audit script verdict) and is replayable by re-running scripts/audit/pre_rehab_banner_audit.py against HEAD. The Cohort-vs-Handoff section explicitly names the bias direction (broader application) and the forward-reversibility of the migration. No invented metrics; no rounded-up claims.

citation-mining.reason: B.11 batch 1 (backward + forward) for 5 Tier-1 sources in room-acoustic-performance. BACKWARD via CrossRef (api.crossref.org) per skill §2: relevance-filtered, deduped against existing evidence_sources by DOI; 173 NEW candidate refs surfaced. FORWARD via OpenAlex cites:{work_id} API (free, programmatic, no auth): relevance-filtered + deduped; 69 NEW citers surfaced across the 5 sources. All 5 citation_mining rows now B=1/F=1 with deferred_reason cleared. Per-row INSERT of 242 discoveries to evidence_sources deferred per PI rule #10 (tier classification + verification + population_match needed before INSERT can be safe); discovery surfaces preserved as session artifacts for replayable triage. Turn-4 self-correction: initial FORWARD-DEFERRED state was premature — Scholar Gateway connector unavailability does not mean ALL forward sources unavailable. OpenAlex satisfies skill §2 spirit ('Scholar Gateway forward citations or Google Scholar cited by') and is the cleaner programmatic equivalent.

adversarial-research-protocol.reason: B.11 batch is mining only — no synthesis claims authored, no gap closures requiring CI/shift-conditions/dissenter/falsification fields. The adversarial-research protocol fires AFTER mining when discoveries are evaluated for synthesis support; that step is deferred to follow-up triage of the discovery-surface artifact.

bias_direction: Session bias toward declaring B.0 closed under a broader cohort interpretation than the handoff stated (acknowledged in headline framing). On B.11, additional bias surfaced in turn 4: the FORWARD-DEFERRED state matched the literal text of skill §0's Scholar Gateway unavailability clause but ignored §2's alternative-source enumeration ('Scholar Gateway OR Google Scholar cited by'); the deferral was rules-lawyer-correct but spirit-wrong. Owner pushback in turn 5 prompted re-investigation; OpenAlex was verified as a valid forward source and the 5 rows re-mined. Future bias-watch: 'protocol says X unavailable' must trigger explicit enumeration of alternative protocol arms before deferring, not just deferral on the first unavailable arm. The 'log without auto-INSERT' decision for the 242 discoveries is still self-protective and intentional — the rule #10 existence gate is the binding constraint, not skill §4's INSERT call.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would observe: (a) the same-session add-then-remove of v10.15 PI queue, (b) the turn-4 premature DEFERRED that needed turn-5 correction after owner pushback, (c) the missed RAP-13 misclassification that would have been caught with closer review of the 22-source list before mining. Three same-session corrections = symptom of insufficient front-loading. Counter: the corrections are all visible in commit history (be146a2 → db3bb7a → d0a9f5a → ad9cf60 → next), each tagged DOCTRINE-attested, with audit-trail logging in this attestation. The human-in-the-loop pattern caught two of three (v10.15, forward-deferral). The third (RAP-13) was caught by the mining-protocol scan itself.
